Stub implementations for regular timer functions used in testing.

# Purpose
The code provides a set of functions for managing regular timer callbacks. It includes functions to initialize the timer system and to add or remove callbacks that are triggered at regular intervals. The functions [`regular_timer_add_seconds_callback`](<#regular_timer_add_seconds_callback>) and [`regular_timer_add_multisecond_callback`](<#regular_timer_add_multisecond_callback>) allow the user to schedule callbacks to be executed every second or every specified number of seconds, respectively. Similarly, [`regular_timer_add_minutes_callback`](<#regular_timer_add_minutes_callback>) and [`regular_timer_add_multiminute_callback`](<#regular_timer_add_multiminute_callback>) schedule callbacks for execution every minute or every specified number of minutes. The [`regular_timer_remove_callback`](<#regular_timer_remove_callback>) function removes a previously scheduled callback, and [`regular_timer_is_scheduled`](<#regular_timer_is_scheduled>) checks if a callback is currently scheduled.

The code is part of a larger system, as indicated by the inclusion of the header file `services/common/regular_timer.h`. It is likely intended to be used as a library or module within a larger application, providing a specific functionality related to time-based event scheduling. The functions use a `RegularTimerInfo` structure, which is not defined in the provided code, suggesting that it is defined elsewhere, possibly in the included header file. The code does not define a main function, indicating that it is not an executable but rather a component to be integrated into other software.
